text: My husband added this to our pool yesterday. He told me not to order this brand anymore. It had several bits of plastic in with the calcium that he did not like having to sift though. I bought this directly from the manufacturer to control mites and aphids on my Red Lake Currant Bush and my Akane Apple tree. Although I am religious about pruning them in the winter and doing at least 3 dormant oil sprays until late February, I can't seem to control the rapid proliferation of these pests come spring. Part of the problem is that you must simultaneously destroy the ants as well as they have a symbiotic relationship with aphids, doing everything possible to ensure aphid survival so they can feed off aphid "milk".

I can't say I'm fond of this product or that it's effective. Perhaps if it weren't so difficult to apply I'd have better success. But one must use 3 cups (yes, you read that correctly) per gallon of water which means wearing a mask to put in the sprayer which then clogs up. Perhaps an agitator would help? And it does no good to just cover the plant; one must hand turn each leaf to get the UNDER side where the beasties dwell or it's useless.

It's super messy too. I manage to get it all over me, the windows, the walkways, etc. And as soon as it rains or you water, you must re-apply. I've had better luck either handpicking the infested leaves and destroying them or spraying with a forceful stream of water. Less messy too. So not sure what to do with the other 20+ pounds of it I have.
Works well combined with a multiple zap strategy:  slug bait, salt water, skewer.  Much more economical to purchase this way rather than at my local garden supply store.

I am still amazed that this worked as well as it did. I have recommended this product to many friends and family with the same issue we had.  those tiny little ants that come each spring. I hate them! sorry, but I do. I find them walking on my counters, floors, etc. and they were everywhere. we had tried everything and nothing worked. Except for this! we bought the type that goes outside and sticks into the ground. we surrounded our home with them. then we purchased the ant traps which were truly amazing because this you can see with your eyes and they came in droids to it and many were stuck inside of the liquid which is very very sticky! unfortunately, my dog was a little more curious than she should have been and moved it and some of the gel came onto my bathroom floor. I didn't know this as the liquid gel inside is clear. it wasn't until I noticed so many black dots on my floor and went to inspect it more closely only to find that those dots were all dead ants! it took a little more elbow grease to remove the gel from the floor. but this is worth every penny, just not to have them on my counters and everywhere I turned. we even purchased the powder! so we bought the traps, the powder, and the ground inserts! we armed ourselves! but no ants and happy customers.

Pros: They work, if you cut the shell off.
Cons: A pain to use, not actually environmentally friendly, kills plants if you do it wrong.

I used these for all my starts last year. I was trying to move away from peat, because it's a non-renewable resource. So of course it turns out these are wrapped in plastic. :-(

The outer fabric shell, which is usually paper that can be buried once the plant is started, is a tough plasticky material, probably a polyester. It looks and feels like paper, but all my plants were fine up to a point, then sad 2 months later, then dead. When I turned up the soil, the little white shells were completely intact, preventing any roots from growing. They decayed into the soil exactly none.

You have to cut off the shell, and when you do the medium crumbles, so you're basically holding a bare plant. They expanded okay and had an acceptable germination rate, hence the 2 stars, but they're (1) a hassle, (2) don't work the way others do, and (3) plastic. (Or something similarly not biodegradable, at least not quickly.)

Initially I was very impressed by the compact design and features of this CTECHi 320Wh LiFeP04 portable power station. There are many things to like about it. However, the power station I received in my order is no longer charging properly after a little over 2 months of usage from the time I received it in the mail. It doesn't charge or show any sign of charging when I use any type C cable in the PD port. I've used several different type C cables that I know work properly and tried different power sources. The power station shows a charging indicator when I plug in the AC adapter, but it doesn't actually charge since the battery charging indication doesn't progress past 1 bar like it normally would. After 4+ hours of being plugged into the AC adapter this power station shows an empty battery indicator when I attempted to power it on multiple times. This power station has never been dropped or had any major physical damage. The ports on the power station look normal to me with no sign of damage. As stated earlier I've only been using this power station for a little over 2 months and it is well within the 24 month warranty policy from the date of purchase. The power station comes with a 12 month warranty from CTECHi, but CTECHi offers a "free one year warranty extension program" as described in the paperwork that came with the item. You do need to contact CTECHi to get the one year warranty extension activated. I've contacted CTECHi regarding this power station's charging issues and will update this review later.

***UPDATES:
8/10/22 - I contacted CTECHi through Amazon messages regarding the power station's charging issues.
8/11/22 - CTECHi replied offering to replace the power station with a new one and requested more details. I provided the requested information and now waiting for the replacement to be shipped to me. I received a tracking number for the shipment, but the package has not been received by FedEx yet.
8/12/22 - CTECHi sent me a message via Amazon apologizing for the inconvenience and informed me that I should receive the replacement in the mail on 8/14/22.
8/14/22 - Received the package in the mail via Fedex Ground.
8/15/22 - I messaged CTECHi asking if this new replacement power station will have the 24 month warranty coverage. I received a reply back within an hour stating yes, the 24 month warranty starts from the date I received this replacement in the mail.

I'm impressed by CTECHi's customer service and fast response to my questions and concerns. The warranty replacement was an easy process and I received my new replacement within 4 days from the date I initially contacted them regarding my original power station's charging issues. It's great that they shipped me a new unit with all of the accessories in the box.

I'm still disappointed the CTECHi 320Wh portable power station I originally received in my order only lasted a little over 2 months. Hopefully this new replacement will last 10 years, which is the advertised product life on the Amazon product listing page. Time will tell. I'll report back several months from now and let everyone know how the power station is doing.

I've changed my original review rating of 1 star to 4 stars due to CTECHi's great customer service, their easy warranty process, they sent me a new replacement in box with all the accessories, and their willingness to take care of their customer in a timely manner.

Please DO NOT mistake these for the old ORTHO brand white plastic, catch & contain mouse trap that they are fashioned after. Those ORTHO traps worked fantastically but they are no longer made so I tried these. Big mistake!
I loaded the traps up with my favorite bait, peanut butter (worked great in the past). Set them along walls where the mice would run. And then watched them do absolutely nothing. No evidence that a mouse ever made any attempt to enter them. When I looked at the size of hole the mouse had to want to go into I realized that our local field mice would find it a tight fit, unless it was a young mouse.
Luckily my 4-pawed mouse catcher, Aspen, can still do a great job at 10 years of age. She went right to work neutralizing those lost field mice.
I won't be buying these again, or any other Tomcat product. They tried to copy something, but could not deliver a good product. They didn't pay attention to details that were important. I wish ORTHO still made those traps so I could get more but for now I will stock up on cat treats (for a job well done last night) and stick with Aspen (sorry, she is not for hire).

I'll start out with the what I like about this Dexpole 100W portable foldable solar panel.
What I liked:
- This solar panel looks nice.
- The handle bar is comfortable to hold and feels solid.
- I like the bi-fold design.
- It comes with a lot of adapters.
- The cables are a good length.
- The solar charger controller has 4 charging ports.

What I didn't like:
- After several tests on different sunny days with clear skies the maximum watts I saw using this solar panel was 55 watts. Average watts I saw was mid 40's on a very clear, sunny day in the afternoon hours. I tested the solar panel using two portable power stations. I tested this solar panel along with two other 100W solar panels and the other two solar panels consistently showed readings of high 70's to low 80's Watt compared to this panel's mid 40's to low 50's Watts readings.
- The shiny reflective clasps holding the solar panel together gets easily scratched.
- This solar panel's support legs are not adjustable. Other solar panels I compared it to have adjustable length straps on the support legs. It would be nice if this one had them.
- During testing this solar panel fell over on days where it was windy.

I requested an exchange for another one from Amazon because I thought the low wattage readings may be due to a defective solar panel. Unfortunately, the replacement Dexpole solar panel I received in the mail got very similar/ comparable results to the original Dexpole solar panel. The price was very reasonable when I bought it on sale, but the solar charging results were disappointing compared to other 100W solar panels.

Please, please, please use something else, folks!  This stuff is EXTREMELY toxic to honeybees.  Only one bee has to get it on its feet, take it back to the hive, and the whole hive dies.  There are plenty of alternatives around that are better for you, the earth, and your pocketbook, such as: insecticidal soaps for aphids, boric acid and sugar for ant control, vinegar, Diatomaceous Earth, Spinosad, Bacillus thuringiensis, etc.  PLEASE do your research!  The honeybees are disappearing at alarming rates due to us humans, including introducing the Varroa Mite and our overuse of pesticides.  Honestly folks, you don't have to bring out the nukes just because you find a couple of ants or aphids in your yard.

I ordered some nitro-coated seeds from another company and also these from Eretz to try out.
I coated my yard with a sprinkling of peat moss across the yard where there are more noticeable bald spots (i was afraid i sprinkled too much of a layer of peat moss on top of the seeds in some spots so I also sprinkled some seeds on top of the peat moss afterwards and lightly got them into the soil by spraying into the peat moss on a 'shower' setting from my hose).
I got really good germination rates from these seeds and covered alot of area with the 5lbs I bought.
I like how you can just throw onto the soil and they basically self-spread without need for a seed-spreader since the seeds are very round n' bouncy.

I can eat these with food much easier than other chilis and won't be destroyed in the bathroom the next day at work :). They def have some heat, just the perfect amount to be a daily eater.
I would say out of the 22 varieties I grew, these prob would be in top 3.

This doesn’t work in my carpet. I had one like it, and I loved it. The “teeth” started moving, and it wasn’t picking up anything. Silicone or rubber ones work a little better. Wish I had my old one, or it was made to really rake a carpet.
They were packaged very well.  They arrived healthy, in good condition, and lots of blooms!

Out of the box, it looked like a solid tool. It came with an adapter fitting for the handle so I could hook it up to my power washer. Problem i had was that the washer fitting it came with is glued into the handle fitting (or may as well be). Maybe the manufacturer's idea of a joke. It wouldn't detach.
I'll never know if it works or not. I spent an hour and a half messing with it and never even sprayed a drop of water from it. An hour and half more than I should have.

Did the recommended vinegar cleaning and no plastic taste.  Live in Midwest Texas, with lots of sun, but it only got up to a maximum of 300 degrees in mid-August with outdoor temps near 100.  Was a little disappointed as some mentioned higher temps; but still amazing using only the sun and plenty hot to cook anything within a reasonable time.  Works best if you are available to adjust the angle from time to time.  Much lower overall temp on a day with intermittent clouds so wouldn't trust meat on those days but breads and veg should be fine.
Very difficult to move the lever from on to off, but sprays well.

First of all we were really suffering assembling this thing. It took us hrs to get it completed.
My second issue is the cushions.
We already have a set of Hanover Orleans ..two swivel chairs which we got last year. We absolutely love them. Excellent quality. In red berry.
Unfortunately this couch in Navy blue doesn't have the same quality. Cushions are much cheaper looking and very stiff. Not comfortable at all.
Makes me sad to return this thing after all our hard work putting it together. We can't sit on it for more than 20 minutes. Look at my picture. Cushions look slimmer in blue.
These cups/pots were not the best quality After few weeks they developed mold. I am trying not to buy plastic but I guess will have to stick to plastic pots next time.

Plastic bottle cruahed (indented) inward while the sprayer was suctioning the liquid out during spraying. I can't get it back to it's normal shape. Other than that, I really liked it. One use was all I got.
not high quality but over all they are nice.
Really nice grill cover. Not sure why they have to make the skirt on it all the way to the ground. Hopefully will keep the sea spray off of it from the ocean.
Easy to install. We installed this on our fig and apricot trees. It was effective against most birds, but not all.

It sounds like this would be a good product --- if only you could use it.  As many people have pointed out in their reviews, the product will not come out of the bottle and mix with the water.  I read the directions and hooked everything up correctly.  I do a lot of spraying, so I am familiar with this application technique.  I spent about an hour of my time trying to get it to work.  My husband looked at it and could not find a solution.  Bayer had better go back to the drawing board because this will effect their brand image.
Fast shipping.. Product as advertised.
screen fell apart within 2 months, useless! save your money and buy one that will last a little longer. This thing is beyond disposable.

I purchased 3 and only 1 worked. I thought the kitchen would be better because that’s where they mostly hang out. Nope I switched containers around and it didn’t work. I don’t even have fruits out. Go figure!! Not happy with this product. Apparently these fruit flies are smarter than the product.
Slow starters. Probably seeds are not fresh. After one week of waiting only few little plants came up

Bought this to try to attract blue Jay's to combat a wasp issue. As others have pointed out the top when latched leaves a big gap that animals could get stuck in. I used a twist tie to close it up (it was a bit of a hastle) I scattered peanuts all over my kitchen floor more than once trying to close it up properly.

As for its effectiveness, the squirrels loved it... One in particular. I watched it all day long take one peanut at a time off to who knows where until it was empty. Have yet to see any birds on it.

Like the title says, if you put all 4 trays in then you will have them tilt 1/4 inch from the edges inwards. This means half if your trays will not be touching water and half the trays will be deep in the water. To me this was a significant drawback from giving this product 5 stars. The only option is to use only 2 trays at a time, which wastes a lot of space, but oh well I don't have that many seeds anyway..
The hanging system is not very stable.  I would not have brought this product if I had seen it in person first.

We bought this so the sensor can go in our utility closet so we don't end up with frozen pipes. It seems to work well, and it replaces one we bought in 2015. The numbers (as in readings) mostly agree so it is a worthy replacement. As far as weatherproof we are using it inside so I don't know, LOL
They get moldy very easily even without much water. I don’t water it for a hot minute but they still get moldy. Why?

Appears to be heavy duty material. No plastic or chemical smell to cover like some mugs. Fit is ok - could be a few inches longer to protect from blowing snow. Test will be to see if the color stands up to summer sun and the material does not crack in the cold like the original Spirit cover.

I lost my first pair and ordered again.  The second one broke after just a couple uses.  Fortunately I found my old well-used one and am still using it.  Maybe they've gotten flimsier or maybe it was a fluke.  Don't know.  I'd have rated the old ones a 5 star, the new ones a 2.  So I split the difference and went with 3.
Bought it to hold up a bird feeder. It does not do the job. Perhaps a small latern might work with this.
